section_text_parts: FastAPI added support for Annotated (and started recommending it) in version 0.95.0.
If you have an older version, you would get errors when trying to use Annotated .
Make sure you Upgrade the FastAPI version to at least 0.95.1 before using Annotated .
tutorial/dependencies/index::section-000.section_text:
FastAPI added support for Annotated (and started recommending it) in version 0.95.0.

If you have an older version, you would get errors when trying to use Annotated .

Make sure you Upgrade the FastAPI version to at least 0.95.1 before using Annotated .

section_text_parts: You'll see what other "things", apart from functions, can be used as dependencies in the next chapter.
tutorial/dependencies/index::section-000.section_text: You'll see what other "things", apart from functions, can be used as dependencies in the next chapter.

section_text_parts: Notice that you don't have to create a special class and pass it somewhere to FastAPI to "register" it or anything similar.
You just pass it to Depends and FastAPI knows how to do the rest.
tutorial/dependencies/index::section-000.section_text:
Notice that you don't have to create a special class and pass it somewhere to FastAPI to "register" it or anything similar.

You just pass it to Depends and FastAPI knows how to do the rest.

section_text_parts: This is just standard Python, it's called a "type alias", it's actually not specific to FastAPI .
But because FastAPI is based on the Python standards, including Annotated , you can use this trick in your code. 😎
tutorial/dependencies/index::section-000.section_text:
This is just standard Python, it's called a "type alias", it's actually not specific to FastAPI .

But because FastAPI is based on the Python standards, including Annotated , you can use this trick in your code. 😎

section_text_parts: If you don't know, check the Async: "In a hurry?" section about async and await in the docs.
tutorial/dependencies/index::section-000.section_text: If you don't know, check the Async: "In a hurry?" section about async and await in the docs.
